headlines.title: High Street mini-marts selling cocaine, cannabis and prescription drugs, BBC secret filming reveals
headlines.description: Across the UK, shopfronts are being exploited by criminal gangs pushing illegal drugs, experts say.

headlines.title: Watch: BBC goes undercover at mini-mart selling drugs
headlines.description: BBC UK editor Ed Thomas confronts a shopkeeper secretly filmed selling cannabis and cocaine to one of our researchers.

headlines.title: Riot police to deploy on French beaches under new deal to stop illegal Channel migrant crossings
headlines.description: The three-year agreement will see at least 50 riot-trained police officers drafted in to tackle violence and “hostile crowds”.

headlines.title: Lebanon accuses Israel of targeting journalist killed in air strike
headlines.description: Lebanon's prime minister accused Israel of war crimes after IDF attacks on Red Cross vehicles also stopped rescuers from reaching the site.

headlines.title: The furious debate over building houses on golf courses
headlines.description: Are golf courses the right places to build housing, or are they simply a soft target?

headlines.title: Ban 'forever chemicals' in uniforms and frying pans, MPs urge
headlines.description: School uniforms and non-stick pans are some of the everyday products that should stop using chemicals called PFAS, MPs say.

headlines.title: 'My instinct was to help him': Runners help exhausted man finish Boston Marathon
headlines.description: The BBC spoke to Boston marathon runner, Ajay Haridasse, who was helped over the finish line by two strangers.

headlines.title: UK borrowing lowest for three years  but Iran war clouds outlook
headlines.description: The improvement in government finances is unlikely to last, analysts say, with the impact of the Iran way yet to hit.

headlines.title: Girl, 14, found in singer D4vd's car died of 'multiple injuries'
headlines.description: The medical examiner said the 14-year-old's cause of death was determined months ago but was blocked from release.

headlines.title: Two trains collide head-on in Denmark, leaving five critically hurt
headlines.description: The accident happened early on Thursday at a level crossing north of Copenhagen.

headlines.title: US Navy chief leaving post 'effective immediately', Pentagon says
headlines.description: Reports say Phelan, whose role was largely administrative, left amid tension over US shipbuilding.

headlines.title: Stars line red carpet at London premiere of Devil Wears Prada 2
headlines.description: Meryl Streep, Anne Hathaway, Stanley Tucci and Emily Blunt were among the actors who appeared on the red carpet.

headlines.title: Labour MPs are increasingly restless – but aren't ready to oust Starmer yet
headlines.description: Labour MPs say that while the prime minister is unpopular, a contest is unlikely as there isn't a candidate ready to replace him.

headlines.title: The Papers: 'PM isolated' and 'Job concerns deepen'
headlines.description: The Lord Mandelson vetting row is featured on many of Thursday's front pages.

headlines.title: 'I can't stop using it' - under-16s have their say on possible social media ban
headlines.description: Thirty three children discussed possible limits the government are considering on social media.

headlines.title: China weathered Trump's tariffs - but the Iran war is taking a toll
headlines.description: The Middle East conflict is putting pressure on factory orders, costs and jobs in China's export-driven economy.

headlines.title: 'My baby scratches and scratches': Families say their homes are making their children sick
headlines.description: A cross-party report has called for safer conditions for the record number of families living in temporary accommodation.

headlines.title: Your pint could come with a surprising health benefit
headlines.description: Beer provides "substantial levels" of vitamin B6 into your diet, according to new research.

headlines.title: 'It's like someone waved a magic wand': Girl, 6, has sight restored by gene therapy
headlines.description: Saffie's mum says Luxturna therapy at Great Ormond Street has been like "someone waved a magic wand".

headlines.title: PM's ex-chief of staff to give evidence on Mandelson vetting
headlines.description: The prime minister's former chief of staff will face questions about his role in the appointment of Lord Mandelson.

headlines.title: Pet owners hit with steep bills after EU passport rule change
headlines.description: Rule change affects dogs, cats and ferrets, with some owners telling the BBC new health certificates cost hundreds of pounds.

headlines.title: I'm on six different NHS waiting lists - it's taking over my life
headlines.description: Amy-Jane Davies is one of 713,048 waiting for any type of NHS treatment in Wales.

headlines.title: Lonely veterans' activity fund spent on Royal British Legion building upgrade
headlines.description: A branch in County Armagh was awarded £140,000 from a charity to deliver three projects for veterans, but internal reports show that only £41,000 went on these schemes.

headlines.title: Advantage Man City? Guardiola leads team to Premier League summit
headlines.description: It took just five minutes for chants of "we are top of the league" to ring out around Turf Moor, as the title pendulum continues to shift towards Manchester City.

headlines.title: AI is already leading to fewer jobs for young people, says Sunak
headlines.description: The former prime minister said graduates' concerns about getting entry-level jobs are justified.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Keir Starmer v The Civil Service
headlines.description: What effect has the Mandelson saga had on the PM’s relationship with the civil service?

headlines.title: Democrats fight back against Trump in Virginia… and win
headlines.description: Has Trump’s plan to redraw America’s political map backfired?

headlines.title: Discover the surprising wildlife of London
headlines.description: Sir David Attenborough explores the surprising wildlife of his hometown – London.

headlines.title: Advantage Man City? 'Queen bee' Guardiola leads them to summit
headlines.description: It took just five minutes for chants of "we are top of the league" to ring out around Turf Moor, as the title pendulum continues to shift towards Manchester City.

headlines.title: Respecting the ball, losing the players - inside Rosenior's Chelsea reign
headlines.description: Liam Rosenior lasted just 106 days at Chelsea. So what went wrong? And how did it go wrong so quickly?

headlines.title: How Russell is surprising himself in F1 title race
headlines.description: George Russell tells BBC Sport how he would enjoy more competition for the F1 title and why his approach to racing is the same as when he was 10 years old.

headlines.title: Best friends with Hamilton - the man 'ruffling feathers' in fencing
headlines.description: BBC Sport speaks to Miles Chamley-Watson, the man behind the World Fencing League, which launches on Saturday in Los Angeles.

headlines.title: Mid-race seizure to marathon debut - Warner Judd's second chance
headlines.description: Great Britain's Jess Warner Judd will make her London Marathon debut less than two years after a mid-race seizure left her fearing her career might be over.
